todos.content: Step 1: Orient in the codebase - identify project structure and Upload model location
Step 2: Map the Upload contract and shared behavior
Step 3: Trace upload creation and processing flow
Step 4: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract
Step 5: Trace identity guards and URL/path resolvers
Step 6: Assess blast radius and rank by risk
Step 7: Produce change + verification map

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest/sha1"
4: 
5: class Upload < ActiveRecord::Base
6:   include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper
7:   include HasUrl
8: 
9:   SHA1_LENGTH = 40
10:   SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD = 0
11:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/original/\dX[/\.\w]*/(\h+)[\.\w]*)}
12:   MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS = 5
13:   DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
14:   # the maximum length of a base62 encoded sha1
15:   MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH = 27
16: 
17:   belongs_to :user
18:   belongs_to :access_control_post, class_name: "Post"
19: 
20:   # when we access this post we don't care if the post
21:   # is deleted
22:   def access_control_post
23:     Post.unscoped { super }
24:   end
25: 
26:   has_many :post_hotlinked_media, dependent: :destroy, class_name: "PostHotlinkedMedia"
27:   has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy
28:   has_many :optimized_videos, dependent: :destroy
29:   has_many :optimized_video_uploads, through: :optimized_videos, source: :optimized_upload
30:   has_many :user_uploads, dependent: :destroy
31:   has_many :upload_references, dependent: :destroy
32:   has_many :posts, through: :upload_references, source: :target, source_type: "Post"
33:   has_many :topic_thumbnails
34:   has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify
35:   after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job, if: :should_convert_video?
36: 
37:   attr_accessor :for_group_message
38:   attr_accessor :for_theme
39:   attr_accessor :for_private_message
40:   attr_accessor :for_export
41:   attr_accessor :for_site_setting
42:   attr_accessor :for_gravatar
43:   attr_accessor :site_setting_name
44:   attr_accessor :validate_file_size
45:   attr_accessor :skip_video_conversion
46: 
47:   validates :filesize, presence: true
48:   validates :original_filename, presence: true
49:   validates :dominant_color, length: { is: 6 }, allow_blank: true
50: 
51:   validates_with UploadValidator
52: 
53:   before_destroy do
54:     UserProfile.where(card_background_upload_id: self.id).update_all(card_background_upload_id: nil)
55:     UserProfile.where(profile_background_upload_id: self.id).update_all(
56:       profile_background_upload_id: nil,
57:     )
58:   end
59: 
60:   after_destroy do
61:     User.where(uploaded_avatar_id: self.id).update_all(uploaded_avatar_id: nil)
62:     UserAvatar.where(gravatar_upload_id: self.id).update_all(gravatar_upload_id: nil)
63:     UserAvatar.where(custom_upload_id: self.id).update_all(custom_upload_id: nil)
64:   end
65: 
66:   scope :by_users, -> { where("uploads.id > ?", SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD) }
67: 
68:   scope :without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status,
69:         -> do
70:           where.not(verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed])
71:         end
72: 
73:   scope :with_invalid_etag_verification_status,
74:         -> { where(verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag]) }
75: 
76:   def self.verification_statuses
77:     @verification_statuses ||=
78:       Enum.new(
79:         unchecked: 1,
80:         verified: 2,
81:         invalid_etag: 3, # Used by S3Inventory to mark S3 Upload records that have an invalid ETag value compared to the ETag value of the inventory file
82:         s3_file_missing_confirmed: 4, # Used by S3Inventory to skip S3 Upload records that are confirmed to not be backed by a file in the S3 file store
83:       )
84:   end
85: 
86:   def self.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
87:     sha1.presence.try { |_| find_by(sha1:) } || get_from_url(url)
88:   end
89: 
90:   def self.mark_invalid_s3_uploads_as_missing
91:     Upload.with_invalid_etag_verification_status.update_all(
92:       verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
93:     )
94:   end
95: 
96:   def self.add_unused_callback(&block)
97:     (@unused_callbacks ||= []) << block
98:   end
99: 
100:   def self.unused_callbacks
101:     @unused_callbacks
102:   end
103: 
104:   def self.reset_unused_callbacks
105:     @unused_callbacks = []
106:   end
107: 
108:   def self.add_in_use_callback(&block)
109:     (@in_use_callbacks ||= []) << block
110:   end
111: 
112:   def self.in_use_callbacks
113:     @in_use_callbacks
114:   end
115: 
116:   def self.reset_in_use_callbacks
117:     @in_use_callbacks = []
118:   end
119: 
120:   def self.with_no_non_post_relations
121:     self.joins(
122:       "LEFT JOIN upload_references ur ON ur.upload_id = uploads.id AND ur.target_type != 'Post'",
123:     ).where("ur.upload_id IS NULL")
124:   end
125: 
126:   def initialize(*args)
127:     super
128:     self.validate_file_size = true
129:   end
130: 
131:   def to_s
132:     self.url
133:   end
134: 
135:   def to_markdown
136:     UploadMarkdown.new(self).to_markdown
137:   end
138: 
139:   def thumbnail(width = self.thumbnail_width, height = self.thumbnail_height)
140:     optimized_images.find_by(width: width, height: height)
141:   end
142: 
143:   def has_thumbnail?(width, height)
144:     thumbnail(width, height).present?
145:   end
146: 
147:   def create_thumbnail!(width, height, opts = nil)
148:     return unless SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
149:     opts ||= {}
150: 
151:     save(validate: false) if get_optimized_image(width, height, opts)
152:   end
153: 
154:   # this method attempts to correct old incorrect extensions
155:   def get_optimized_image(width, height, opts = nil)
156:     opts ||= {}
157: 
158:     fix_image_extension if (!extension || extension.length == 0)
159: 
160:     opts = opts.merge(raise_on_error: true)
161:     begin
162:       OptimizedImage.create_for(self, width, height, opts)
163:     rescue => ex
164:       Rails.logger.info ex if Rails.env.development?
165:       opts = opts.merge(raise_on_error: false)
166:       if fix_image_extension
167:         OptimizedImage.create_for(self, width, height, opts)
168:       else
169:         nil
170:       end
171:     end
172:   end
173: 
174:   def content
175:     original_path = Discourse.store.path_for(self)
176: 
177:     if original_path.blank?
178:       File.read(Discourse.store.download!(self))
179:     else
180:       File.read(original_path)
181:     end
182:   end
183: 
184:   def fix_image_extension
185:     return false if extension == "unknown"
186: 
187:     begin
188:       # this is relatively cheap once cached
189:       original_path = Discourse.store.path_for(self)
190:       original_path = Discourse.store.download(self) if original_path.blank?
191: 
192:       image_info =
193:         begin
194:           image = FastImage.new(original_path)
195:           image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
196:           image
197:         rescue StandardError
198:           nil
199:         end
200:       new_extension = image_info&.type&.to_s || "unknown"
201: 
202:       if new_extension != self.extension
203:         self.update_columns(extension: new_extension)
204:         true
205:       end
206:     rescue StandardError
207:       self.update_columns(extension: "unknown")
208:       true
209:     end
210:   end
211: 
212:   def destroy
213:     Upload.transaction do
214:       Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)
215:       super
216:     end
217:   end
218: 
219:   def short_url
220:     "upload://#{short_url_basename}"
221:   end
222: 
223:   def uploaded_before_secure_uploads_enabled?
224:     original_sha1.blank?
225:   end
226: 
227:   def matching_access_control_post?(post)
228:     access_control_post_id == post.id
229:   end
230: 
231:   def copied_from_other_post?(post)
232:     return false if access_control_post_id.blank?
233:     !matching_access_control_post?(post)
234:   end
235: 
236:   def short_path
237:     self.class.short_path(sha1: self.sha1, extension: self.extension)
238:   end
239: 
240:   def self.consider_for_reuse(upload, post)
241:     return upload if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || upload.blank? || post.blank?
242:     if !upload.matching_access_control_post?(post) || upload.uploaded_before_secure_uploads_enabled?
243:       return nil
244:     end
245:     upload
246:   end
247: 
248:   def self.secure_uploads_url?(url)
249:     # we do not want to exclude topic links that for whatever reason
250:     # have secure-uploads in the URL e.g. /t/secure-uploads-are-cool/223452
251:     route = UrlHelper.rails_route_from_url(url)
252:     return false if route.blank?
253:     route[:action] == "show_secure" && route[:controller] == "uploads" &&
254:       FileHelper.is_supported_media?(url)
255:   rescue ActionController::RoutingError
256:     false
257:   end
258: 
259:   def self.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(url, include_content_disposition:)
260:     route = UrlHelper.rails_route_from_url(url)

295:   def fix_dimensions!
296:     return if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}")
297: 
298:     begin
299:       path =
300:         if local?
301:           Discourse.store.path_for(self)
302:         else
303:           Discourse.store.download!(self)
304:         end
305: 
306:       if extension == "svg"
307:         w, h =
308:           begin
309:             Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
310:               "identify",
311:               "-ping",
312:               "-format",
313:               "%w %h",
314:               path,
315:               timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
316:             ).split(" ")
317:           rescue StandardError
318:             [0, 0]
319:           end
320:       else
321:         w, h = FastImage.new(path, raise_on_failure: true).size
322:       end
323: 
324:       self.width = w || 0
325:       self.height = h || 0
326: 
327:       self.thumbnail_width, self.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
328: 
329:       self.update_columns(
330:         width: width,
331:         height: height,
332:         thumbnail_width: thumbnail_width,
333:         thumbnail_height: thumbnail_height,
334:       )
335:     rescue => e
336:       Discourse.warn_exception(e, message: "Error getting image dimensions")
337:     end
338:     nil
339:   end

341:   # on demand image size calculation, this allows us to null out image sizes
342:   # and still handle as needed
343:   def get_dimension(key)
344:     if v = self[key]
345:       return v
346:     end
347:     fix_dimensions!
348:     self[key]
349:   end

353:   end

361:   end

364:     get_dimension(:thumbnail_height)
365:   end

367:   def dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: false)
368:     val = read_attribute(:dominant_color)
369:     if val.nil? && calculate_if_missing
370:       calculate_dominant_color!
371:       read_attribute(:dominant_color)
372:     else
373:       val
374:     end

377:   def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
378:     color = nil
379: 
380:     color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"
381: 
382:     if color.nil?
383:       local_path ||=
384:         if local?
385:           Discourse.store.path_for(self)
386:         else
387:           Discourse.store.download(self)
388:         end
389: 
390:       if local_path.nil?
391:         # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
392:         color = ""
393:       end
394: 
395:       color ||=
396:         begin
397:           data =
398:             Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
399:               "nice",
400:               "-n",
401:               "10",
402:               "convert",
403:               local_path,
404:               "-depth",
405:               "8",
406:               "-resize",
407:               "1x1",
408:               "-define",
409:               "histogram:unique-colors=true",
410:               "-format",
411:               "%c",
412:               "histogram:info:",
413:               timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
414:             )
415: 
416:           # Output format:
417:           # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)
418: 
419:           color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]
420: 
421:           raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?
422: 
423:           color
424:         rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
425:           # Timeout or unable to parse image
426:           # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
427:           # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
428:           ""
429:         end
430:     end
431: 
432:     if persisted?
433:       self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
434:     else
435:       self.dominant_color = color
436:     end
437:   end

439:   def target_image_quality(local_path, test_quality)
440:     @file_quality ||=
441:       begin
442:         Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
443:           "identify",
444:           "-ping",
445:           "-format",
446:           "%Q",
447:           local_path,
448:           timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
449:         ).to_i
450:       rescue StandardError
451:         0
452:       end
453: 
454:     test_quality if @file_quality == 0 || @file_quality > test_quality
455:   end

469:   def self.sha1_from_base62_encoded(encoded_sha1)
470:     return nil if encoded_sha1.length > MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH
471:     sha1 = Base62.decode(encoded_sha1).to_s(16)
472: 
473:     if sha1.length > SHA1_LENGTH
474:       nil
475:     else
476:       sha1.rjust(SHA1_LENGTH, "0")
477:     end
478:   end

492:   def update_secure_status(source: "unknown", override: nil)
493:     if override.nil?
494:       mark_secure, reason = UploadSecurity.new(self).should_be_secure_with_reason
495:     else
496:       mark_secure = override
497:       reason = "manually overridden"
498:     end
499: 
500:     secure_status_did_change = self.secure? != mark_secure
501:     self.update(secure_params(mark_secure, reason, source))
502: 
503:     if secure_status_did_change && Discourse.store.external?
504:       Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(self)
505:       sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
506:     end
507: 
508:     secure_status_did_change
509:   end

511:   def sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
512:     optimized_videos
513:       .includes(:optimized_upload)
514:       .each do |optimized_video|
515:         optimized_upload = optimized_video.optimized_upload
516:         if optimized_upload.secure? != mark_secure
517:           optimized_upload.update_secure_status(
518:             source: "sync_with_original_upload",
519:             override: mark_secure,
520:           )
521:         end
522:       end
523:   end

525:   def secure_params(secure, reason, source = "unknown")
526:     {
527:       secure: secure,
528:       security_last_changed_reason: reason + " | source: #{source}",
529:       security_last_changed_at: Time.zone.now,
530:     }
531:   end

533:   def self.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: nil)
534:     problems = []
535: 
536:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("migrate_upload_to_new_scheme") do
537:       if SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
538:         max_file_size_kb = [
539:           SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
540:           SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
541:         ].max.kilobytes
542: 
543:         local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
544:         db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
545: 
546:         scope =
547:           Upload
548:             .by_users
549:             .where("url NOT LIKE '%/original/_X/%' AND url LIKE ?", "%/uploads/#{db}%")
550:             .order(id: :desc)
551: 
552:         scope = scope.limit(limit) if limit
553: 
554:         if scope.count == 0
555:           SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme = false
556:           return problems
557:         end
558: 
559:         remap_scope = nil
560: 
561:         scope.each do |upload|
562:           begin
563:             # keep track of the url
564:             previous_url = upload.url.dup
565:             # where is the file currently stored?
566:             external = previous_url =~ %r{\A//}
567:             # download if external
568:             if external
569:               url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + previous_url
570: 
571:               begin
572:                 retries ||= 0
573: 
574:                 file =
575:                   FileHelper.download(
576:                     url,
577:                     max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
578:                     tmp_file_name: "discourse",
579:                     follow_redirect: true,
580:                   )
581:               rescue OpenURI::HTTPError
582:                 retry if (retries += 1) < 1
583:                 next
584:               end
585: 
586:               path = file.path
587:             else
588:               path = local_store.path_for(upload)
589:             end
590:             # compute SHA if missing
591:             upload.sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(path) if upload.sha1.blank?
592: 
593:             # store to new location & update the filesize
594:             File.open(path) do |f|
595:               upload.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(f, upload)
596:               upload.filesize = f.size
597:               upload.save!(validate: false)
598:             end
599:             # remap the URLs
600:             DbHelper.remap(UrlHelper.absolute(previous_url), upload.url) unless external
601: 
602:             DbHelper.remap(
603:               previous_url,
604:               upload.url,
605:               excluded_tables: %w[
606:                 posts
607:                 post_search_data
608:                 incoming_emails
609:                 notifications
610:                 single_sign_on_records
611:                 stylesheet_cache
612:                 topic_search_data
613:                 users
614:                 user_emails
615:                 draft_sequences
616:                 optimized_images
617:               ],
618:             )
619: 
620:             remap_scope ||=
621:               begin
622:                 Post
623:                   .with_deleted
624:                   .where(
625:                     "raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/\\d+/' OR raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/original/(\\d|[a-z])/'",
626:                   )
627:                   .select(:id, :raw, :cooked)
628:                   .all
629:               end
630: 
631:             remap_scope.each do |post|
632:               post.raw.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
633:               post.cooked.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
634:               if post.changed?
635:                 Post.with_deleted.where(id: post.id).update_all(raw: post.raw, cooked: post.cooked)
636:               end
637:             end
638: 
639:             upload.optimized_images.find_each(&:destroy!)
640:             upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
641:             # remove the old file (when local)
642:             FileUtils.rm(path, force: true) unless external
643:           rescue => e
644:             problems << { upload: upload, ex: e }
645:           ensure
646:             file&.unlink
647:             file&.close
648:           end
649:         end
650:       end
651:     end
652: 
653:     problems
654:   end

656:   def self.extract_upload_ids(raw)
657:     return [] if raw.blank?
658: 
659:     sha1s = []
660: 
661:     raw.scan(/\/(\h{40})/).each { |match| sha1s << match[0] }
662: 
663:     raw
664:       .scan(%r{/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)})
665:       .each { |match| sha1s << Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(match[0]) }
666: 
667:     Upload.where(sha1: sha1s.uniq).pluck(:id)
668:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module HasUrl
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   class_methods do
7:     def extract_url(url)
8:       url.match(self::URL_REGEX)
9:     end
10: 
11:     def extract_sha1(path)
12:       data = extract_url(path)
13:       return if data.blank?
14: 
15:       sha1 = data[2]
16:       return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
17: 
18:       sha1
19:     end
20: 
21:     def get_from_url(url)
22:       return if url.blank?
23: 
24:       uri =
25:         begin
26:           URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
27:         rescue URI::Error
28:         end
29: 
30:       return if uri&.path.blank?
31:       data = extract_url(uri.path)
32: 
33:       if data.blank?
34:         result = nil
35:         result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
36:         return result
37:       end
38: 
39:       result = nil
40: 
41:       if self.name == "Upload"
42:         sha1 = data[2]
43:         result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
44:       end
45: 
46:       result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
50:       urls = []
51:       sha1s = []
52: 
53:       upload_urls.each do |url|
54:         next if url.blank?
55: 
56:         uri =
57:           begin
58:             URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
59:           rescue URI::Error
60:           end
61: 
62:         next if uri&.path.blank?
63:         urls << uri.path
64: 
65:         if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
66:           urls << data[1]
67:           sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
68:         end
69:       end
70: 
71:       self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
72:     end
73:   end
74: end

3: class UploadReference < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :upload
5:   belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true
6: 
7:   delegate :to_markdown, to: :upload
8: 
9:   def self.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [], target: nil, target_type: nil, target_id: nil)
10:     if !target && !(target_type && target_id)
11:       raise "target OR target_type and target_id are required"
12:     end
13: 
14:     if target.present?
15:       target_type = target.class
16:       target_id = target.id
17:     end
18: 
19:     upload_ids = upload_ids.uniq.reject(&:blank?)
20:     target_type = target_type.to_s
21: 
22:     if upload_ids.empty?
23:       UploadReference.where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id).delete_all
24: 
25:       return
26:     end
27: 
28:     rows =
29:       upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
30:         {
31:           upload_id: upload_id,
32:           target_type: target_type,
33:           target_id: target_id,
34:           created_at: Time.zone.now,
35:           updated_at: Time.zone.now,
36:         }
37:       end
38: 
39:     UploadReference.transaction do |transaction|
40:       UploadReference
41:         .where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id)
42:         .where.not(upload_id: upload_ids)
43:         .delete_all
44: 
45:       UploadReference.insert_all(rows)
46:     end
47:   end
48: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   include HasUrl
5:   belongs_to :upload
6: 
7:   # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
8:   VERSION = 2
9:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
10: 
11:   def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
12:     @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
13:     # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
14:     # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
15:     #
16:     # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
17:     if lock_per_machine?
18:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
19:         DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
20:       end
21:     else
22:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   def self.lock_per_machine?
27:     return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
28:     @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
32:     @lock_per_machine = value
33:   end
34: 
35:   def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
36:     return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
37:     return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
38: 
39:     # no extension so try to guess it
40:     upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
41: 
42:     if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
43:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
44:         raise InvalidAccess
45:       else
46:         # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
47:         return
48:       end
49:     end
50: 
51:     # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
52:     extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
53:     thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
54: 
55:     # correct bad thumbnail if needed
56:     if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
57:       thumbnail.destroy!
58:       thumbnail = nil
59:     end
60: 
61:     return thumbnail if thumbnail
62: 
63:     store = Discourse.store
64: 
65:     # create the thumbnail otherwise
66:     original_path = store.path_for(upload)
67: 
68:     if original_path.blank?
69:       # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
70:       original_path = store.download(upload)
71:     end
72: 
73:     if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
74:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
75:         raise InvalidAccess
76:       else
77:         # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
78:         return
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     lock(upload.id, width, height) do
83:       # may have been generated since we got the lock
84:       thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
85: 
86:       # return the previous thumbnail if any
87:       return thumbnail if thumbnail
88: 
89:       if original_path.blank?
90:         Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
91:       else
92:         # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
93: 
94:         return nil if extension.length == 1
95: 
96:         temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
97:         temp_path = temp_file.path
98: 
99:         target_quality =
100:           upload.target_image_quality(
101:             original_path,
102:             SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
103:           )
104:         opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
105:         opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
106: 
107:         # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
108:         if extension == ".svg"
109:           FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
110:           resized = true
111:         elsif opts[:crop]
112:           resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
113:         else
114:           resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
115:         end
116: 
117:         if resized
118:           # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
119:           thumbnail =
120:             OptimizedImage.create!(
121:               upload_id: upload.id,
122:               sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
123:               extension: extension,
124:               width: width,
125:               height: height,
126:               url: "",
127:               filesize: File.size(temp_path),
128:               version: VERSION,
129:             )
130: 
131:           # store the optimized image and update its url
132:           File.open(temp_path) do |file|
133:             url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
134:             if url.present?
135:               thumbnail.url = url
136:               thumbnail.save
137:             else
138:               Rails.logger.error(
139:                 "Failed to store optimized image of size #{width}x#{height} from url: #{upload.url}\nTemp image path: #{temp_path}",
140:               )
141:             end
142:           end
143:         end
144: 
145:         # close && remove temp file
146:         temp_file.close!
147:       end
148: 
149:       thumbnail
150:     end
151:   end
152: 
153:   def destroy
154:     OptimizedImage.transaction do
155:       Discourse.store.remove_optimized_image(self) if self.upload
156:       super
157:     end
158:   end
159: 
160:   def local?
161:     !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
162:   end
163: 
164:   def calculate_filesize
165:     path =
166:       if local?
167:         Discourse.store.path_for(self)
168:       else
169:         Discourse.store.download!(self)
170:       end
171:     File.size(path)
172:   end
173: 
174:   def filesize
175:     if size = read_attribute(:filesize)
176:       size
177:     else
178:       size = calculate_filesize
179: 
180:       self[:filesize] = size
181:       update_columns(filesize: size) if !new_record?
182:       size
183:     end
184:   end
185: 
186:   def self.safe_path?(path)
187:     # this matches instructions which call #to_s
188:     path = path.to_s
189:     return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
190:     return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m
191:     true
192:   end
193: 
194:   def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)
195:     paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }
196:   end
197: 
198:   IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
199: 
200:   def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
201:     opts ||= {}
202: 
203:     # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
204:     # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
205:     # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
206:     # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
207:     # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
208:     extension = nil
209:     if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
210:       extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
211:     else
212:       extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
213:     end
214: 
215:     if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
216:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
217:     end
218:     "#{extension}:#{path}"
219:   end
220: 
221:   def self.thumbnail_or_resize
222:     SiteSetting.strip_image_metadata ? "thumbnail" : "resize"
223:   end
224: 
225:   def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
226:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
227: 
228:     # note FROM my not be named correctly
229:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
230:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
231: 
232:     instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
233: 
234:     instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
235: 
236:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
237: 
238:     # NOTE: ORDER is important!
239:     instructions.concat(
240:       %W[
241:         -auto-orient
242:         -gravity
243:         center
244:         -background
245:         transparent
246:         -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
247:         #{dimensions}^
248:         -extent
249:         #{dimensions}
250:         -interpolate
251:         catrom
252:         -unsharp
253:         2x0.5+0.7+0
254:         -interlace
255:         none
256:         -profile
257:         #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
258:         #{to}
259:       ],
260:     )
261:   end
262: 
263:   def self.crop_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
264:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
265: 
266:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
267:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
268: 
269:     instructions = %W{
270:       convert
271:       #{from}[0]
272:       -auto-orient
273:       -gravity
274:       north
275:       -background
276:       transparent
277:       -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
278:       #{dimensions}^
279:       -crop
280:       #{dimensions}+0+0
281:       -unsharp
282:       2x0.5+0.7+0
283:       -interlace
284:       none
285:       -profile
286:       #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
287:     }
288: 
289:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
290: 
291:     instructions << to
292:   end
293: 
294:   def self.downsize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
295:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
296: 
297:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
298:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
299: 
300:     %W{
301:       convert
302:       #{from}[0]
303:       -auto-orient
304:       -gravity
305:       center
306:       -background
307:       transparent
308:       -interlace
309:       none
310:       -resize
311:       #{dimensions}
312:       -profile
313:       #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
314:       #{to}
315:     }
316:   end
317: 
318:   def self.resize(from, to, width, height, opts = {})
319:     optimize("resize", from, to, "#{width}x#{height}", opts)
320:   end
321: 
322:   def self.crop(from, to, width, height, opts = {})
323:     optimize("crop", from, to, "#{width}x#{height}", opts)
324:   end
325: 
326:   def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
327:     optimize("downsize", from, to, dimensions, opts)
328:   end
329: 
330:   def self.optimize(operation, from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
331:     method_name = "#{operation}_instructions"
332: 
333:     instructions = self.public_send(method_name.to_sym, from, to, dimensions, opts)
334:     convert_with(instructions, to, opts)
335:   end
336: 
337:   MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE = 500_000
338:   MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS = 20
339: 
340:   def self.convert_with(instructions, to, opts = {})
341:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
342:       "nice",
343:       "-n",
344:       "10",
345:       *instructions,
346:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS,
347:     )
348: 
349:     allow_pngquant = to.downcase.ends_with?(".png") && File.size(to) < MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE
350:     FileHelper.optimize_image!(to, allow_pngquant: allow_pngquant)
351:     true
352:   rescue => e
353:     if opts[:raise_on_error]
354:       raise e
355:     else
356:       error = +"Failed to optimize image:"
357: 
358:       if e.message =~ /\Aconvert:([^`]+)/
359:         error << $1
360:       else
361:         error << " unknown reason"
362:       end
363: 
364:       Discourse.warn(
365:         error,
366:         upload_id: opts[:upload_id],
367:         location: to,
368:         error_message: e.message,
369:         instructions: instructions,
370:       )
371:       false
372:     end
373:   end
374: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class OptimizedVideo < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :upload
5:   belongs_to :optimized_upload, class_name: "Upload"
6: 
7:   validates :upload_id, presence: true
8:   validates :optimized_upload_id, presence: true
9:   validates :adapter, presence: true
10:   validates :upload_id, uniqueness: { scope: :adapter }
11: 
12:   def self.create_for(upload, filename, user_id, options = {})
13:     return if upload.blank?
14: 
15:     optimized_upload =
16:       Upload.create!(
17:         user_id: user_id,
18:         original_filename: filename,
19:         filesize: options[:filesize],
20:         sha1: options[:sha1],
21:         extension: options[:extension] || "mp4",
22:         url: options[:url],
23:         etag: options[:etag],
24:         skip_video_conversion: true,
25:         secure: upload.secure?,
26:       )
27: 
28:     optimized_video =
29:       OptimizedVideo.new(
30:         upload_id: upload.id,
31:         optimized_upload_id: optimized_upload.id,
32:         adapter: options[:adapter],
33:       )
34: 
35:     if optimized_video.save
36:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [optimized_upload.id], target: upload)
37:       optimized_video
38:     else
39:       optimized_upload.destroy
40:       Rails.logger.error(
41:         "Failed to create optimized video for upload ID #{upload.id}: #{optimized_video.errors.full_messages.join(", ")}",
42:       )
43:       nil
44:     end
45:   end
46: 
47:   def destroy
48:     OptimizedVideo.transaction do
49:       Discourse.store.remove_upload(optimized_upload) if optimized_upload
50:       if optimized_upload_id
51:         UploadReference.where(upload_id: optimized_upload_id, target: upload).destroy_all
52:       end
53:       super
54:       optimized_upload&.destroy
55:     end
56:   end
57: 
58:   delegate :url, :filesize, :sha1, :extension, to: :optimized_upload
59: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This model indicates an 'attempt' to create a topic thumbnail
4: # for an upload. This means we don't keep trying to create optimized
5: # images for small/invalid original images.
6: #
7: # Foreign keys with ON DELETE CASCADE are used to ensure unneeded data
8: # is deleted automatically
9: class TopicThumbnail < ActiveRecord::Base
10:   belongs_to :upload
11:   belongs_to :optimized_image
12: 
13:   def self.find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)
14:     existing =
15:       TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
16:     return existing if existing
17:     return nil if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
18: 
19:     target_width, target_height =
20:       ImageSizer.resize(
21:         original.width,
22:         original.height,
23:         { max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height },
24:       )
25: 
26:     needs_optimization = target_width < original.width && target_height < original.height
27:     skip_animated = SiteSetting.animated_topic_thumbnails && original.animated?
28: 
29:     if needs_optimization && !skip_animated
30:       optimized = OptimizedImage.create_for(original, target_width, target_height)
31:     end
32: 
33:     # may have been associated already, bulk insert will skip dupes
34:     TopicThumbnail.insert_all(
35:       [
36:         upload_id: original.id,
37:         max_width: max_width,
38:         max_height: max_height,
39:         optimized_image_id: optimized&.id,
40:       ],
41:     )
42: 
43:     TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
44:   end
45: 
46:   def self.ensure_consistency!
47:     # Clean up records for broken upload links or broken optimized image links
48:     TopicThumbnail
49:       .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads on upload_id = uploads.id")
50:       .joins("LEFT JOIN optimized_images on optimized_image_id = optimized_images.id")
51:       .where(<<~SQL)
52:         (optimized_image_id IS NOT NULL AND optimized_images IS NULL)
53:         OR uploads IS NULL
54:       SQL
55:       .delete_all
56: 
57:     # Delete records for sizes which are no longer needed
58:     sizes =
59:       Topic.thumbnail_sizes +
60:         ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
61:     sizes_sql =
62:       sizes.map { |s| "(max_width = #{s[0].to_i} AND max_height = #{s[1].to_i})" }.join(" OR ")
63:     TopicThumbnail.where.not(sizes_sql).delete_all
64:   end
65: end
66:

72: #  upload_id          :bigint           not null

80: #  index_topic_thumbnails_on_upload_id           (upload_id)
81: #  unique_topic_thumbnails                       (upload_id,max_width,max_height) UNIQUE

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "fastimage"
4: 
5: class UploadCreator
6:   TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)
7: 
8:   ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
9:     circle
10:     clipPath
11:     defs
12:     ellipse
13:     feGaussianBlur
14:     filter
15:     g
16:     line
17:     linearGradient
18:     marker
19:     path
20:     polygon
21:     polyline
22:     radialGradient
23:     rect
24:     stop
25:     style
26:     svg
27:     text
28:     textPath
29:     tref
30:     tspan
31:     use
32:   ].each(&:freeze)
33: 
34:   # Available options
35:   #  - type (string)
36:   #  - origin (string)
37:   #  - for_group_message (boolean)
38:   #  - for_theme (boolean)
39:   #  - for_private_message (boolean)
40:   #  - pasted (boolean)
41:   #  - for_export (boolean)
42:   #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
43:   #  - skip_validations (boolean)
44:   def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
45:     @file = file
46:     @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
47:     @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
48:     @opts = opts
49:     @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
50:     @opts[:validate] = (
51:       if opts[:skip_validations].present?
52:         !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
53:       else
54:         true
55:       end
56:     )
57:   end
58: 
59:   def create_for(user_id)
60:     if filesize <= 0
61:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
62:       return @upload
63:     end
64: 
65:     @image_info =
66:       begin
67:         image = FastImage.new(@file)
68:         image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
69:         image
70:       rescue StandardError
71:         nil
72:       end
73:     is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
74:     is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
75:     is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
76:     is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"
77: 
78:     # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
79:     # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
80:     # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
81:     # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
82:     # in S3
83:     #
84:     # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
85:     # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
86:     # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
87:     # place. Needs a refactor.
88:     external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
89:     sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file
90: 
91:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
92:       # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
93:       if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
94:         convert_heif!
95:         is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
96:       end
97: 
98:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
99:         extract_image_info!
100:         return @upload if @upload.errors.present?
101: 
102:         if @image_info.type == :svg
103:           clean_svg!
104:         elsif @image_info.type == :ico
105:           convert_favicon_to_png!
106:         elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
107:           convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
108:           fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
109:           crop! if should_crop?
110:           optimize! if should_optimize?
111:           downsize! if should_downsize?
112:           return @upload if is_still_too_big?
113:         end
114: 
115:         # conversion may have switched the type
116:         image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
117:       end
118: 
119:       # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
120:       # which is only used for secure uploads
121:       sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
122:       unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
123:         external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail
124: 
125:       # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
126:       # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
127:       # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
128:       # access/permission issues for uploads
129:       # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
130:       # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
131:       # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
132:       if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
133:         # do we already have that upload?
134:         @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
135: 
136:         # make sure the previous upload has not failed
137:         if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
138:           @upload.destroy
139:           @upload = nil
140:         end
141: 
142:         # return the previous upload if any
143:         if @upload
144:           add_metadata!
145:           UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
146:           return @upload
147:         end
148:       end
149: 
150:       fixed_original_filename = nil
151: 
152:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
153:         current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
154:         expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
155: 
156:         # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
157:         # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
158:         # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
159:         if current_extension != expected_extension
160:           basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
161:           fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
162:         end
163:       end
164: 
165:       # create the upload otherwise
166:       @upload = Upload.new
167:       @upload.user_id = user_id
168:       @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
169:       @upload.filesize = filesize
170:       @upload.sha1 =
171:         (
172:           if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
173:             unique_hash
174:           else
175:             sha1
176:           end
177:         )
178:       @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
179:       @upload.url = ""
180:       @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
181:       @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]
182: 
183:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
184:         if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
185:           w, h = [0, 0]
186: 
187:           # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
188:           # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
189:           # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
190:           # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
191:           begin
192:             w, h =
193:               Discourse::Utils
194:                 .execute_command(
195:                   "identify",
196:                   "-ping",
197:                   "-format",
198:                   "%w %h",
199:                   "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
200:                   timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
201:                 )
202:                 .split(" ")
203:                 .map(&:to_i)
204:           rescue StandardError
205:             # use default 0, 0
206:           end
207:         else
208:           w, h = @image_info.size
209:         end
210: 
211:         @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
212:         @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
213:         @upload.animated = animated?
214:         @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)
215:       end
216: 
217:       add_metadata!
218: 
219:       if SiteSetting.secure_uploads
220:         secure, reason =
221:           UploadSecurity.new(@upload, @opts.merge(creating: true)).should_be_secure_with_reason
222:         attrs = @upload.secure_params(secure, reason, "upload creator")
223:         @upload.assign_attributes(attrs)
224:       end
225: 
226:       # Callbacks using this event to validate the upload or the file must add errors to the
227:       # upload errors object.
228:       #
229:       # Can't do any validation of the file if external_upload_too_big because we don't have
230:       # the actual file.
231:       if !external_upload_too_big
232:         DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, @file, is_image, @upload, @opts[:validate])
233:       end
234:       return @upload unless @upload.errors.empty? && @upload.save(validate: @opts[:validate])
235: 
236:       should_move = false
237:       upload_changed =
238:         if external_upload_too_big
239:           false
240:         else
241:           Upload.generate_digest(@file) != sha1_before_changes
242:         end
243: 
244:       store = Discourse.store
245: 
246:       if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key] && store.external?
247:         should_move = external_upload_too_big || !upload_changed
248:       end
249: 
250:       if should_move
251:         # move the file in the store instead of reuploading
252:         url =
253:           store.move_existing_stored_upload(
254:             existing_external_upload_key: @opts[:existing_external_upload_key],
255:             upload: @upload,
256:           )
257:       else
258:         # store the file and update its url
259:         File.open(@file.path) { |f| url = store.store_upload(f, @upload) }
260:         if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
261:           store.delete_file(@opts[:existing_external_upload_key])
262:         end
263:       end
264: 
265:       if url.present?
266:         @upload.url = url
267:         @upload.save!(validate: @opts[:validate])
268:       else
269:         @upload.errors.add(
270:           :url,
271:           I18n.t("upload.store_failure", upload_id: @upload.id, user_id: user_id),
272:         )
273:       end
274: 
275:       if @upload.errors.empty? && is_image && @opts[:type] == "avatar" && @upload.extension != "svg"
276:         Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: @upload.id)
277:       end
278: 
279:       if @upload.errors.empty?
280:         UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
281:       end
282: 
283:       @upload
284:     end
285:   ensure
286:     if @file
287:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
288:     end
289:   end
290: 
291:   def extract_image_info!
292:     @image_info =
293:       begin
294:         image = FastImage.new(@file)
295:         image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
296:         image
297:       rescue StandardError
298:         nil
299:       end
300:     @file.rewind
301: 
302:     if @image_info.nil?
303:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.not_supported_or_corrupted"))
304:     elsif filesize <= 0
305:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
306:     elsif pixels == 0 && @image_info.type.to_s != "svg"
307:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.size_not_found"))
308:     elsif max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
309:       @upload.errors.add(
310:         :base,
311:         I18n.t(
312:           "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
313:           max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
314:           original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
315:         ),
316:       )
317:     end
318:   end
319: 
320:   MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG = 1280 * 720
321: 
322:   def convert_png_to_jpeg?
323:     return false unless @image_info.type == :png
324:     return true if @opts[:pasted]
325:     return false if SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality == 100
326:     pixels > MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG
327:   end
328: 
329:   MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED = 75_000
330:   MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO = 0.70
331: 
332:   def convert_favicon_to_png!
333:     png_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .png])
334: 
335:     from = @file.path
336:     to = png_tempfile.path
337: 
338:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
339: 
340:     from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
341:     to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
342: 
343:     from = "#{from}[-1]" # We only want the last(largest) image of the .ico file
344: 
345:     opts = { flatten: false } # Preserve transparency
346: 
347:     begin
348:       execute_convert(from, to, opts)
349:     rescue StandardError
350:       # retry with debugging enabled
351:       execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
352:     end
353: 
354:     @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
355:     @file = png_tempfile
356:     extract_image_info!
357:   end
358: 
359:   def convert_to_jpeg!
360:     return if @opts[:for_site_setting]
361:     return if filesize < MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
362: 
363:     jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
364: 
365:     from = @file.path
366:     to = jpeg_tempfile.path
367: 
368:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
369: 
370:     from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
371:     to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
372: 
373:     opts = {}
374: 
375:     desired_quality = [
376:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality,
377:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality,
378:     ].compact.min
379: 
380:     target_quality = @upload.target_image_quality(from, desired_quality)
381:     opts = { quality: target_quality } if target_quality
382: 
383:     begin
384:       execute_convert(from, to, opts)
385:     rescue StandardError
386:       # retry with debugging enabled
387:       execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
388:     end
389: 
390:     new_size = File.size(jpeg_tempfile.path)
391: 
392:     keep_jpeg = new_size < filesize * MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO
393:     keep_jpeg &&= (filesize - new_size) > MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
394: 
395:     if keep_jpeg
396:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
397:       @file = jpeg_tempfile
398:       extract_image_info!
399:     else
400:       jpeg_tempfile.close!
401:     end
402:   end
403: 
404:   def convert_heif_to_jpeg?
405:     File.extname(@filename).downcase.match?(/\.hei(f|c)\z/)
406:   end
407: 
408:   def convert_heif!
409:     jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
410:     from = @file.path
411:     to = jpeg_tempfile.path
412:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
413: 
414:     begin
415:       execute_convert(from, to)
416:     rescue StandardError
417:       # retry with debugging enabled
418:       execute_convert(from, to, { debug: true })
419:     end
420: 
421:     @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
422:     @file = jpeg_tempfile
423:     extract_image_info!
424:   end
425: 
426:   MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS = 20
427:   def execute_convert(from, to, opts = {})
428:     command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]
429:     command << "-flatten" unless opts[:flatten] == false
430:     command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
431:     command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
432:     command << to
433: 
434:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
435:       *command,
436:       failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
437:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
438:     )
439:   end
440: 
441:   def should_alter_quality?
442:     return false if animated?
443: 
444:     desired_quality =
445:       (
446:         if @image_info.type == :png
447:           SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality
448:         else
449:           SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality
450:         end
451:       )
452: 
453:     @upload.target_image_quality(@file.path, desired_quality).present?
454:   end
455: 
456:   def should_downsize?
457:     max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size && !animated?
458:   end
459: 
460:   def downsize!
461:     3.times do
462:       original_size = filesize
463:       down_tempfile = Tempfile.new(["down", ".#{@image_info.type}"])
464: 
465:       from = @file.path
466:       to = down_tempfile.path
467: 
468:       OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
469: 
470:       OptimizedImage.downsize(from, to, "50%", scale_image: true, raise_on_error: true)
471: 
472:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
473:       @file = down_tempfile
474: 
475:       extract_image_info!
476: 
477:       return if filesize >= original_size || pixels == 0 || !should_downsize?
478:     end
479:   rescue StandardError
480:     @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.optimize_failure_message"))
481:   end
482: 
483:   def is_still_too_big?
484:     if max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
485:       @upload.errors.add(
486:         :base,
487:         I18n.t(
488:           "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
489:           max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
490:           original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
491:         ),
492:       )
493:       true
494:     elsif max_image_size > 0 && filesize >= max_image_size
495:       @upload.errors.add(
496:         :base,
497:         I18n.t(
498:           "upload.images.too_large_humanized",
499:           max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_image_size),
500:         ),
501:       )
502:       true
503:     else
504:       false
505:     end
506:   end

508:   def clean_svg!
509:     doc = Nokogiri.XML(@file)
510:     doc.xpath(svg_allowlist_xpath).remove
511:     doc.xpath("//@*[starts-with(name(), 'on')]").remove
512:     doc
513:       .css("use")
514:       .each do |use_el|
515:         if use_el.attr("href")
516:           use_el.remove_attribute("href") unless use_el.attr("href").starts_with?("#")
517:         end
518:         use_el.remove_attribute("xlink:href")
519:       end
520: 
521:     File.write(@file.path, doc.to_s)
522:     @file.rewind
523:   end

525:   def should_fix_orientation?
526:     # orientation is between 1 and 8, 1 being the default
527:     # cf. http://www.daveperrett.com/articles/2012/07/28/exif-orientation-handling-is-a-ghetto/
528:     @image_info.orientation.to_i > 1
529:   end

531:   MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME = 5
532:   def fix_orientation!
533:     path = @file.path
534: 
535:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(path)
536:     path = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(path, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
537: 
538:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
539:       "magick",
540:       path,
541:       "-auto-orient",
542:       path,
543:       timeout: MAX_FIX_ORIENTATION_TIME,
544:     )
545: 
546:     extract_image_info!
547:   end
548: 
549:   def should_crop?
550:     if %w[profile_background card_background custom_emoji].include?(@opts[:type]) && animated?
551:       return false
552:     end
553: 
554:     TYPES_TO_CROP.include?(@opts[:type])
555:   end
556: 
557:   def crop!
558:     max_pixel_ratio = Discourse::PIXEL_RATIOS.max
559:     filename_with_correct_ext = "image.#{@image_info.type}"
560: 
561:     case @opts[:type]
562:     when "avatar"
563:       width = height = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
564:       OptimizedImage.resize(
565:         @file.path,
566:         @file.path,
567:         width,
568:         height,
569:         filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
570:       )
571:     when "profile_background"
572:       max_width = 850 * max_pixel_ratio
573:       width, height =
574:         ImageSizer.resize(
575:           @image_info.size[0],
576:           @image_info.size[1],
577:           max_width: max_width,
578:           max_height: max_width,
579:         )
580:       OptimizedImage.downsize(
581:         @file.path,
582:         @file.path,
583:         "#{width}x#{height}\>",
584:         filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
585:       )
586:     when "card_background"
587:       max_width = 590 * max_pixel_ratio
588:       width, height =
589:         ImageSizer.resize(
590:           @image_info.size[0],
591:           @image_info.size[1],
592:           max_width: max_width,
593:           max_height: max_width,
594:         )
595:       OptimizedImage.downsize(
596:         @file.path,
597:         @file.path,
598:         "#{width}x#{height}\>",
599:         filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
600:       )
601:     when "custom_emoji"
602:       OptimizedImage.downsize(
603:         @file.path,
604:         @file.path,
605:         "100x100\>",
606:         filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
607:       )
608:     end
609: 
610:     extract_image_info!
611:   end
612: 
613:   def should_optimize?
614:     # GIF is too slow (plus, we'll soon be converting them to MP4)
615:     # Optimizing SVG is useless
616:     return false if @file.path =~ /\.(gif|svg)\z/i
617:     # Safeguard for large PNGs
618:     return pixels < 2_000_000 if @file.path =~ /\.png/i
619:     # Everything else is fine!
620:     true
621:   end
622: 
623:   def optimize!
624:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)
625:     FileHelper.optimize_image!(@file.path)
626:     extract_image_info!
627:   end
628: 
629:   def filesize
630:     @filesize || File.size?(@file.path).to_i
631:   end
632: 
633:   def max_image_size
634:     @max_image_size ||= SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
635:   end
636: 
637:   def max_image_pixels
638:     @max_image_pixels ||= SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels * 1_000_000
639:   end
640: 
641:   def pixels
642:     @image_info.size&.reduce(:*).to_i
643:   end
644: 
645:   def svg_allowlist_xpath
646:     @@svg_allowlist_xpath ||=
647:       "//*[#{ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS.map { |e| "name()!='#{e}'" }.join(" and ")}]"
648:   end

650:   def add_metadata!
651:     @upload.for_private_message = true if @opts[:for_private_message]
652:     @upload.for_group_message = true if @opts[:for_group_message]
653:     @upload.for_theme = true if @opts[:for_theme]
654:     @upload.for_export = true if @opts[:for_export]
655:     @upload.for_site_setting = true if @opts[:for_site_setting]
656:     @upload.site_setting_name = @opts[:site_setting_name] if @opts[:site_setting_name]
657:     @upload.for_gravatar = true if @opts[:for_gravatar]
658:   end

662:   def animated?
663:     return @animated if @animated != nil
664: 
665:     @animated ||=
666:       begin
667:         is_animated = FastImage.animated?(@file)
668:         type = @image_info.type.to_s
669: 
670:         if is_animated != nil
671:           # FastImage will return nil if it cannot determine if animated
672:           is_animated
673:         elsif %w[gif webp avif].include?(type)
674:           # Only GIFs, WEBPs and a few other unsupported image types can be animated
675:           OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(@file.path)
676: 
677:           command = ["identify", "-ping", "-format", "%n\\n", @file.path]
678:           frames =
679:             begin
680:               Discourse::Utils.execute_command(*command, timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS).to_i
681:             rescue StandardError
682:               1
683:             end
684: 
685:           frames > 1
686:         else
687:           false
688:         end
689:       end
690:   end

118:     def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
119:       depth = get_depth_for(id)
120:       tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")
121:       "#{type}/#{depth + 1}X/#{tree}#{sha}#{extension}"
122:     end
123: 
124:     def get_path_for_upload(upload)
125:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
126:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
127:       if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
128:         return prefix_path(path)
129:       end
130: 
131:       extension =
132:         if upload.extension
133:           ".#{upload.extension}"
134:         else
135:           # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
136:           File.extname(upload.original_filename)
137:         end
138: 
139:       get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
140:     end
141: 
142:     def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
143:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
144:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
145:       if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
146:         return prefix_path(path)
147:       end
148: 
149:       upload = optimized_image.upload
150:       version = optimized_image.version || 1
151:       extension =
152:         "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
153:       get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
154:     end
155: 
156:     CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
157:     CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
158:     CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100
159: 
160:     def get_cache_path_for(filename)
161:       "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
162:     end
163: 
164:     def get_from_cache(filename)
165:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
166:       path if File.exist?(path)
167:     end
168: 
169:     def cache_file(file, filename)
170:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
171:       dir = File.dirname(path)
172:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
173:       FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)
174: 
175:       DiskCacheEviction.evict(
176:         dir: CACHE_DIR,
177:         max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
178:         evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
179:       )
180:     end
181: 
182:     private
183: 
184:     def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
185:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
186:         name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
187:         extension = File.extname(name)
188:         filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
189: 
190:         unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
191:           max_file_size_kb ||= [
192:             SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
193:             SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
194:           ].max.kilobytes
195: 
196:           if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
197:             url =
198:               Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
199:           else
200:             url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
201:           end
202: 
203:           url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
204: 
205:           file =
206:             FileHelper.download(
207:               url,
208:               max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
209:               tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
210:               follow_redirect: true,
211:             )
212: 
213:           return if file.nil?
214: 
215:           cache_file(file, filename)
216:         end
217: 
218:         get_from_cache(filename)
219:       end
220:     end
221: 
222:     def not_implemented
223:       raise "Not implemented."
224:     end
225: 
226:     def get_depth_for(id)
227:       depths = [0]
228:       depths << Math.log(id / 1_000.0, 16).ceil if id.positive?
229:       depths.max
230:     end
231: 
232:     def prefix_path(path)
233:       path
234:     end
235:   end
236: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "uri"
4: require "mini_mime"
5: require "file_store/base_store"
6: require "s3_helper"
7: require "file_helper"
8: 
9: module FileStore
10:   class S3Store < BaseStore
11:     TOMBSTONE_PREFIX = "tombstone/"
12: 
13:     delegate :abort_multipart,
14:              :presign_multipart_part,
15:              :list_multipart_parts,
16:              :complete_multipart,
17:              to: :s3_helper
18: 
19:     def initialize(s3_helper = nil)
20:       @s3_helper = s3_helper
21:     end
22: 
23:     def s3_helper
24:       @s3_helper ||=
25:         S3Helper.new(
26:           s3_bucket,
27:           Rails.configuration.multisite ? multisite_tombstone_prefix : TOMBSTONE_PREFIX,
28:           use_accelerate_endpoint: SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_transfer_acceleration,
29:         )
30:     end
31: 
32:     def store_upload(file, upload, content_type = nil)
33:       upload.url = nil
34:       path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
35:       url, upload.etag =
36:         store_file(
37:           file,
38:           path,
39:           filename: upload.original_filename,
40:           content_type: content_type,
41:           cache_locally: true,
42:           private: upload.secure?,
43:           upload_id: upload.id,
44:         )
45:       url
46:     end
47: 
48:     def move_existing_stored_upload(existing_external_upload_key:, upload: nil, content_type: nil)
49:       upload.url = nil
50:       path = get_path_for_upload(upload)
51:       url, upload.etag =
52:         store_file(
53:           nil,
54:           path,
55:           filename: upload.original_filename,
56:           content_type: content_type,
57:           cache_locally: false,
58:           private: upload.secure?,
59:           move_existing: true,
60:           existing_external_upload_key: existing_external_upload_key,
61:         )
62:       url
63:     end
64: 
65:     def store_optimized_image(file, optimized_image, content_type = nil, secure: false)
66:       optimized_image.url = nil
67:       path = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
68:       url, optimized_image.etag =
69:         store_file(file, path, content_type: content_type, private: secure)
70:       url
71:     end
72: 
73:     # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
74:     #
75:     # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
76:     # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
77:     # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
78:     #
79:     # options
80:     #   - filename
81:     #   - content_type
82:     #   - cache_locally
83:     #   - move_existing
84:     #   - private
85:     #   - existing_external_upload_key
86:     def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
87:       path = path.dup
88: 
89:       filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
90:       # cache file locally when needed
91:       cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
92: 
93:       cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
94:       if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
95:         cache_control =
96:           "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
97:       end
98: 
99:       options = {
100:         cache_control: cache_control,
101:         content_type:
102:           opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
103:       }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
104: 
105:       # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
106:       # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
107:       # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
108:       options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
109: 
110:       path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
111: 
112:       # if this fails, it will throw an exception
113:       if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
114:         original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
115:         options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
116:         path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
117:         delete_file(original_path)
118:       else
119:         path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
120:       end
121: 
122:       # return the upload url and etag
123:       [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
124:     end
125: 
126:     def delete_file(path)
127:       # delete the object outright without moving to tombstone,
128:       # not recommended for most use cases
129:       s3_helper.delete_object(path)
130:     end
131: 
132:     def remove_file(url, path)
133:       return unless has_been_uploaded?(url)
134:       # copy the removed file to tombstone
135:       s3_helper.remove(path, true)
136:     end
137: 
138:     def copy_file(source:, destination:, secure:)
139:       s3_helper.copy(source, destination, options: default_s3_options(secure:))
140:     end
141: 
142:     def has_been_uploaded?(url)
143:       return false if url.blank?
144: 
145:       begin
146:         parsed_url = URI.parse(UrlHelper.encode(url))
147:       rescue StandardError
148:         # There are many exceptions possible here including Addressable::URI:: exceptions
149:         # and URI:: exceptions, catch all may seem wide, but it makes no sense to raise ever
150:         # on an invalid url here
151:         return false
152:       end
153: 
154:       base_hostname = URI.parse(absolute_base_url).hostname
155:       if url[base_hostname]
156:         # if the hostnames match it means the upload is in the same
157:         # bucket on s3. however, the bucket folder path may differ in
158:         # some cases, and we do not want to assume the url is uploaded
159:         # here. e.g. the path of the current site could be /prod and the
160:         # other site could be /staging
161:         if s3_bucket_folder_path.present?
162:           return parsed_url.path.starts_with?("/#{s3_bucket_folder_path}")
163:         else
164:           return true
165:         end
166:       end
167: 
168:       return false if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
169: 
170:       s3_cdn_url = URI.parse(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url || "")
171:       cdn_hostname = s3_cdn_url.hostname
172: 
173:       if cdn_hostname.presence && url[cdn_hostname] &&
174:            (s3_cdn_url.path.blank? || parsed_url.path.starts_with?(s3_cdn_url.path))
175:         return true
176:       end
177:       false
178:     end
179: 
180:     def s3_bucket_folder_path
181:       S3Helper.get_bucket_and_folder_path(s3_bucket)[1]
182:     end
183: 
184:     def s3_bucket_name
185:       S3Helper.get_bucket_and_folder_path(s3_bucket)[0]
186:     end
187: 
188:     def absolute_base_url
189:       @absolute_base_url ||= SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url
190:     end
191: 
192:     def s3_upload_host
193:       SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.presence || "https:#{absolute_base_url}"
194:     end
195: 
196:     def external?
197:       true
198:     end
199: 
200:     def purge_tombstone(grace_period)
201:       s3_helper.update_tombstone_lifecycle(grace_period)
202:     end
203: 
204:     def multisite_tombstone_prefix
205:       File.join("uploads", "tombstone", RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db, "/")
206:     end
207: 
208:     def download_url(upload)
209:       return unless upload
210:       "#{upload.short_path}?dl=1"
211:     end
212: 
213:     def path_for(upload)
214:       url = upload&.url
215:       FileStore::LocalStore.new.path_for(upload) if url && url[%r{\A/[^/]}]
216:     end
217: 
218:     def url_for(upload, force_download: false)

353:     def copy_from(source_path)
354:       local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
355:       public_upload_path = File.join(local_store.public_dir, local_store.upload_path)
356: 
357:       # The migration to S3 and lots of other code expects files to exist in public/uploads,
358:       # so lets move them there before executing the migration.
359:       if public_upload_path != source_path
360:         if Dir.exist?(public_upload_path)
361:           old_upload_path = "#{public_upload_path}_#{SecureRandom.hex}"
362:           FileUtils.mv(public_upload_path, old_upload_path)
363:         end
364:       end
365: 
366:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.expand_path("..", public_upload_path))
367:       FileUtils.symlink(source_path, public_upload_path)
368: 
369:       FileStore::ToS3Migration.new(
370:         s3_options: FileStore::ToS3Migration.s3_options_from_site_settings,
371:         migrate_to_multisite: Rails.configuration.multisite,
372:       ).migrate
373:     ensure
374:       FileUtils.rm(public_upload_path) if File.symlink?(public_upload_path)
375:       FileUtils.mv(old_upload_path, public_upload_path) if old_upload_path
376:     end

383:     # The following are canned ACLs defined by AWS S3 and not some generic value which we decide to use.

397:     def self.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: true)
398:       return if !SiteSetting.s3_enable_access_control_tags
399: 
400:       key = SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_key
401:       return if key.blank?
402: 
403:       option_value = {
404:         key =>
405:           (
406:             if secure
407:               SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_private_value
408:             else
409:               SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_public_value
410:             end
411:           ),
412:       }
413: 
414:       encode_form ? URI.encode_www_form(option_value) : option_value
415:     end

418:       options = {}
419: 
420:       if acl_value = acl_option_value(secure:)
421:         options[:acl] = acl_value
422:       end
423: 
424:       if tagging_option_value = visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:)
425:         options[:tagging] = tagging_option_value
426:       end
427: 
428:       options

435:     # S3 limits total request header/metadata size to 2 KB. Content-Disposition
436:     # includes the filename twice (filename= and filename*=UTF-8''), so we
437:     # limit the full header to 1600 bytes to stay within that budget.
438:     MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES = 1_600
439: 
440:     def self.content_disposition_for(filename, disposition: nil)
441:       disposition ||= FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename) ? "inline" : "attachment"
442:       return "" if filename.blank?
443: 
444:       header = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename:)
445:       return header if header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
446: 
447:       extension = File.extname(filename)
448:       basename = File.basename(filename, extension)
449: 
450:       # Binary search for the longest basename that fits within the limit
451:       low = 1
452:       high = basename.length
453: 
454:       while low < high
455:         mid = (low + high + 1) / 2
456:         candidate = "#{basename[0...mid]}#{extension}"
457:         test_header =
458:           ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename: candidate)
459:         if test_header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
460:           low = mid
461:         else
462:           high = mid - 1
463:         end
464:       end
465: 
466:       ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
467:         disposition:,
468:         filename: "#{basename[0...low]}#{extension}",
469:       )
470:     end
