474:     def presigned_get_url(
475:       url,
476:       force_download: false,
477:       filename: false,
478:       expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds
479:     )
480:       opts = { expires_in: expires_in }
481: 
482:       if filename
483:         disposition =
484:           (force_download || !FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename)) ? "attachment" : "inline"
485:         opts[:response_content_disposition] = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
486:           disposition:,
487:           filename:,
488:         )
489:       end
490: 
491:       obj = object_from_path(url)
492:       obj.presigned_url(:get, opts)
493:     end

503:     def update_access_control(key, secure, remove_existing_acl: false)
504:       acl = self.class.acl_option_value(secure:)
505: 
506:       if acl.present? || remove_existing_acl
507:         begin
508:           object_from_path(key).acl.put(acl:)
509:         rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NotImplemented => err
510:           Discourse.warn_exception(
511:             err,
512:             message: "The file store object storage provider does not support setting ACLs",
513:           )
514:         end
515:       end
516: 
517:       if tagging_option_value =
518:            self.class.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: false)
519:         s3_helper.upsert_tag(
520:           key,
521:           tag_key: tagging_option_value.keys.first,
522:           tag_value: tagging_option_value.values.first,
523:         )
524:       end
525:     rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NoSuchKey
526:       Rails.logger.warn(
527:         "Could not update access control on upload with key: '#{key}'. Upload is missing.",
528:       )
529:     end

531:     def list_missing(model, prefix)
532:       connection = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.raw_connection
533:       connection.exec("CREATE TEMP TABLE verified_ids(val integer PRIMARY KEY)")
534:       marker = nil
535:       files = s3_helper.list(prefix, marker)
536: 
537:       while files.count > 0
538:         verified_ids = []
539: 
540:         files.each do |f|
541:           id = model.where("url LIKE '%#{f.key}' AND etag = '#{f.etag}'").pick(:id)
542:           verified_ids << id if id.present?
543:           marker = f.key
544:         end
545: 
546:         verified_id_clause =
547:           verified_ids.map { |id| "('#{PG::Connection.escape_string(id.to_s)}')" }.join(",")
548:         connection.exec("INSERT INTO verified_ids VALUES #{verified_id_clause}")
549:         files = s3_helper.list(prefix, marker)
550:       end
551: 
552:       missing_uploads =
553:         model.joins("LEFT JOIN verified_ids ON verified_ids.val = id").where(
554:           "verified_ids.val IS NULL",
555:         )
556:       missing_count = missing_uploads.count
557: 
558:       if missing_count > 0
559:         missing_uploads.find_each { |upload| puts upload.url }
560: 
561:         puts "#{missing_count} of #{model.count} #{model.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing"
562:       end
563:     ensure
564:       connection.exec("DROP TABLE verified_ids") unless connection.nil?
565:     end
566:   end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/discourse/lib/upload_security.rb</path>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
5: #
6: # Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
7: # are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
8: # when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
9: # option is present.
10: #
11: # If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
12: # secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
13: # original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
14: # post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
15: # on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
16: # complex queries and joins to perform.
17: #
18: # These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
19: # an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
20: # (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
21: # state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
22: #
23: # NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
24: # secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
25: # does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
26: # efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
27: class UploadSecurity
28:   @@custom_public_types = []
29: 
30:   PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
31:     avatar
32:     custom_emoji
33:     profile_background
34:     card_background
35:     category_logo
36:     category_logo_dark
37:     category_background
38:     group_flair
39:     badge_image
40:     site_setting
41:   ]
42: 
43:   PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
44:     Badge
45:     Category
46:     CustomEmoji
47:     Group
48:     SiteSetting
49:     ThemeField
50:     User
51:     UserAvatar
52:     UserProfile
53:   ]
54: 
55:   def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
56:     @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
57:   end
58: 
59:   # used in tests
60:   def self.reset_custom_public_types
61:     @@custom_public_types = []
62:   end
63: 
64:   def initialize(upload, opts = {})
65:     @upload = upload
66:     @opts = opts
67:     @upload_type = @opts[:type]
68:     @creating = @opts[:creating]
69:   end
70: 
71:   def should_be_secure?
72:     should_be_secure_with_reason.first
73:   end
74: 
75:   def should_be_secure_with_reason
76:     insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
77:     secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
78:       return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
79:       return true, reason if perform_check(check)
80:     end
81: 
82:     [false, "no checks satisfied"]
83:   end
84: 
85:   private
86: 
87:   def access_control_post
88:     @access_control_post ||=
89:       @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
90:   end
91: 
92:   def insecure_context_checks
93:     {
94:       secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
95:       insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
96:       public_type: "upload is public type",
97:       regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
98:       publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
99:     }
100:   end
101: 
102:   def secure_context_checks
103:     {
104:       login_required: "login is required",
105:       access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
106:       secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
107:       uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
108:       already_secure: "upload is already secure",
109:     }
110:   end
111: 
112:   # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
113:   # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
114:   # immediately if there is an access control post.
115:   def priority_check?(check)
116:     check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
117:   end
118: 
119:   def perform_check(check)
120:     send("#{check}_check")
121:   end
122: 
123:   #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
124: 
125:   def secure_uploads_disabled_check
126:     !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
127:   end
128: 
129:   def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
130:     return false if !@creating
131:     @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
132:   end
133: 
134:   def public_type_check
135:     PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
136:   end
137: 
138:   def publicly_referenced_first_check
139:     return false if @creating
140:     first_reference =
141:       @upload
142:         .upload_references
143:         .joins(<<~SQL)
144:           LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
145:         SQL
146:         .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
147:         .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
148:         .first
149:     return false if first_reference.blank?
150:     PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
151:   end
152: 
153:   def regular_emoji_check
154:     return false if @upload.origin.blank?
155:     uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
156:     return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
157:     uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
158:   end
159: 
160:   #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
161: 
162:   #--------------------------#
163: 
164:   #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
165: 
166:   def login_required_check
167:     SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
168:   end
169: 
170:   # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
171:   # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
172:   #
173:   # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
174:   #
175:   # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
176:   # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
177:   #
178:   # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
179:   # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
180:   def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
181:     access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
182:   end
183: 
184:   def uploading_in_composer_check
185:     @upload_type == "composer"
186:   end
187: 
188:   def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
189:     return false if !@creating
190:     @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
191:   end
192: 
193:   def already_secure_check
194:     @upload.secure?
195:   end
196: 
197:   #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
198: end

(End of file - total 198 lines)

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/discourse/lib/validators/upload_validator.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "file_helper"
4: 
5: class UploadValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
6:   def validate(upload)
7:     # staff can upload any file in PM
8:     if (upload.for_private_message && SiteSetting.allow_staff_to_upload_any_file_in_pm)
9:       return true if upload.user&.staff?
10:     end
11: 
12:     # check the attachment blocklist
13:     if upload.for_group_message && SiteSetting.allow_all_attachments_for_group_messages
14:       return upload.original_filename =~ SiteSetting.blocked_attachment_filenames_regex
15:     end
16: 
17:     extension = File.extname(upload.original_filename)[1..-1] || ""
18: 
19:     return validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension) if upload.for_site_setting
20: 
21:     if upload.for_gravatar && FileHelper.supported_gravatar_extensions.include?(extension)
22:       maximum_image_file_size(upload)
23:       return true
24:     end
25: 
26:     return true if changing_upload_security?(upload)
27: 
28:     if is_authorized?(upload, extension)
29:       if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
30:         authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
31:         maximum_image_file_size(upload)
32:       else
33:         authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
34:         maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
35:       end
36:     end
37:   end
38: 
39:   def validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension)
40:     unless upload.user&.staff?
41:       upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.unauthorized"))
42:       return false
43:     end
44: 
45:     setting_opts = site_setting_type_hash(upload.site_setting_name)
46:     authorized_extensions = setting_opts[:authorized_extensions]
47: 
48:     if authorized_extensions.present?
49:       unless extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
50:         upload.errors.add(
51:           :original_filename,
52:           I18n.t("upload.unauthorized", authorized_extensions: authorized_extensions),
53:         )
54:         return false
55:       end
56: 
57:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
58:     else
59:       unless FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
60:         upload.errors.add(:original_filename, I18n.t("upload.images_only"))
61:         return false
62:       end
63: 
64:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
65:     end
66: 
67:     upload.errors.empty?
68:   end
69: 
70:   # this should only be run on existing records, and covers cases of
71:   # upload.update_secure_status being run outside of the creation flow,
72:   # where some cases e.g. have exemptions on the extension enforcement
73:   def changing_upload_security?(upload)
74:     !upload.new_record? &&
75:       upload.changed_attributes.keys.all? do |attribute|
76:         %w[secure security_last_changed_at security_last_changed_reason].include?(attribute)
77:       end
78:   end
79: 
80:   def is_authorized?(upload, extension)
81:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_extensions(upload))
82:   end
83: 
84:   def authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
85:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_images(upload))
86:   end
87: 
88:   def maximum_image_file_size(upload)
89:     maximum_file_size(upload, "image")
90:   end
91: 
92:   def authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
93:     extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_attachments(upload))
94:   end
95: 
96:   def maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
97:     maximum_file_size(upload, "attachment")
98:   end
99: 
100:   private
101: 
102:   def site_setting_type_hash(setting_name)
103:     return {} if setting_name.blank?
104:     SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(setting_name.to_sym)
105:   end
106: 
107:   def validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
108:     return if !upload.validate_file_size
109: 
110:     max_size_kb = setting_opts[:max_file_size_kb]
111: 
112:     if max_size_kb.present?
113:       max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
114:       if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
115:         upload.errors.add(
116:           :filesize,
117:           I18n.t(
118:             "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
119:             max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
120:           ),
121:         )
122:       end
123:     elsif FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
124:       maximum_image_file_size(upload)
125:     else
126:       maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
127:     end
128:   end
129: 
130:   def extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
131:     extensions_to_set(authorized_extensions).include?(extension.downcase)
132:   end
133: 
134:   def extensions_to_set(exts)
135:     extensions = Set.new
136: 
137:     exts
138:       .gsub(/[\s\.]+/, "")
139:       .downcase
140:       .split("|")
141:       .each { |extension| extensions << extension if extension.exclude?("*") }
142: 
143:     extensions
144:   end
145: 
146:   def authorized_extensions(upload)
147:     extensions =
148:       if upload.for_theme
149:         SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
150:       elsif upload.for_export
151:         SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
152:       else
153:         SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
154:       end
155:     extensions_to_set(extensions)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def authorized_images(upload)
159:     authorized_extensions(upload) & FileHelper.supported_images
160:   end
161: 
162:   def authorized_attachments(upload)
163:     authorized_extensions(upload) - FileHelper.supported_images
164:   end
165: 
166:   def authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
167:     if upload.user&.staff?
168:       return true if SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff.include?("*")
169:     end
170:     extensions =
171:       if upload.for_theme
172:         SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
173:       elsif upload.for_export
174:         SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
175:       else
176:         SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
177:       end
178:     extensions.include?("*")
179:   end
180: 
181:   def extension_authorized?(upload, extension, extensions)
182:     return true if authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
183: 
184:     staff_extensions = Set.new
185:     if upload.user&.staff?
186:       staff_extensions = extensions_to_set(SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff)
187:       return true if staff_extensions.include?(extension.downcase)
188:     end
189: 
190:     unless authorized = extensions.include?(extension.downcase)
191:       message =
192:         I18n.t(
193:           "upload.unauthorized",
194:           authorized_extensions: (extensions | staff_extensions).to_a.join(", "),
195:         )
196:       upload.errors.add(:original_filename, message)
197:     end
198: 
199:     authorized
200:   end
201: 
202:   def maximum_file_size(upload, type)
203:     return if !upload.validate_file_size
204: 
205:     max_size_kb =
206:       if upload.for_export
207:         SiteSetting.max_export_file_size_kb
208:       else
209:         if upload.user&.id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID && type == "attachment"
210:           [
211:             SiteSetting.get("system_user_max_attachment_size_kb"),
212:             SiteSetting.get("max_attachment_size_kb"),
213:           ].max
214:         else
215:           SiteSetting.get("max_#{type}_size_kb")
216:         end
217:       end
218: 
219:     max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.kilobytes
220: 
221:     if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
222:       message =
223:         I18n.t(
224:           "upload.#{type}s.too_large_humanized",
225:           max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
226:         )
227:       upload.errors.add(:filesize, message)
228:     end
229:   end
230: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class UploadMarkdown
4:   def initialize(upload)
5:     @upload = upload
6:   end
7: 
8:   def to_markdown(display_name: nil)
9:     if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@upload.original_filename)
10:       image_markdown(display_name: display_name)
11:     elsif FileHelper.is_supported_playable_media?(@upload.original_filename)
12:       playable_media_markdown(display_name: display_name)
13:     else
14:       attachment_markdown(display_name: display_name)
15:     end
16:   end
17: 
18:   def image_markdown(display_name: nil)
19:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
20:     "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{@upload.width}x#{@upload.height}](#{@upload.short_url})"
21:   end
22: 
23:   def attachment_markdown(display_name: nil, with_filesize: true)
24:     human_filesize = with_filesize ? " (#{@upload.human_filesize})" : ""
25:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
26: 
27:     "[#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|attachment](#{@upload.short_url})#{human_filesize}"
28:   end
29: 
30:   def playable_media_markdown(display_name: nil)
31:     type =
32:       if FileHelper.is_supported_audio?(@upload.original_filename)
33:         "audio"
34:       elsif FileHelper.is_supported_video?(@upload.original_filename)
35:         "video"
36:       end
37:     return attachment_markdown if !type
38:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
39:     "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{type}](#{@upload.short_url})"
40:   end
41: 
42:   def self.escape_markdown(text)
43:     text.gsub(/[\\*_~`\[\]|]/) { |c| "\\#{c}" }
44:   end
45: end

43:         )

45:       else
46:         params.require(:upload_type)
47:       end

49:     type = type.parameterize(separator: "_")[0..50]

52:          (
53:            SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_avatar || SiteSetting.auth_overrides_avatar ||
54:              !me.in_any_groups?(SiteSetting.uploaded_avatars_allowed_groups_map)
55:          )
56:       return render json: failed_json, status: :unprocessable_entity
57:     end
58: 
59:     url = params[:url]
60:     file = params[:file] || params[:files]&.first
61:     pasted = params[:pasted] == "true"
62:     for_private_message = params[:for_private_message] == "true"

68:     # note, atm hijack is processed in its own context and has not access to controller
69:     # longer term we may change this
70:     hijack do
71:       begin
72:         info =
73:           UploadsController.create_upload(
74:             current_user: me,
75:             file:,
76:             url:,
77:             type:,
78:             for_private_message:,
79:             for_site_setting:,
80:             site_setting_name:,
81:             pasted:,
82:             is_api:,
83:             retain_hours:,
84:           )
85:       rescue => e
86:         render json: failed_json.merge(message: e.message&.split("\n")&.first),
87:                status: :unprocessable_entity
88:       else
89:         render json: UploadsController.serialize_upload(info), status: Upload === info ? 200 : 422
90:       end
91:     end
92:   end
93: 
94:   def lookup_urls
95:     params.permit(short_urls: [])
96:     uploads = []
97: 
98:     if (params[:short_urls] && params[:short_urls].length > 0)
99:       PrettyText::Helpers
100:         .lookup_upload_urls(params[:short_urls])
101:         .each do |short_url, paths|
102:           uploads << { short_url: short_url, url: paths[:url], short_path: paths[:short_path] }
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106:     render json: uploads.to_json
107:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module HasPostUploadReferences
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   def link_post_uploads(fragments: nil)
7:     upload_ids = []
8: 
9:     each_upload_url(fragments: fragments) do |url, _, sha1|
10:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
11: 
12:       # Link any video thumbnails
13:       if SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && upload.present? &&
14:            FileHelper.supported_video.include?(upload.extension&.downcase)
15:         # Video thumbnails have the filename of the video file sha1 with a .png or .jpg extension.
16:         # This is because at time of upload in the composer we don't know the topic/post id yet
17:         # and there is no thumbnail info added to the markdown to tie the thumbnail to the topic/post after
18:         # creation.
19:         thumbnail =
20:           Upload
21:             .where("original_filename like ?", "#{upload.sha1}.%")
22:             .order(id: :desc)
23:             .first if upload.sha1.present?
24:         if thumbnail.present?
25:           upload_ids << thumbnail.id
26:           handle_video_thumbnail(thumbnail) if respond_to?(:handle_video_thumbnail, true)
27:         end
28:       end
29:       upload_ids << upload.id if upload.present?
30:     end
31: 
32:     upload_references =
33:       upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
34:         {
35:           target_id: self.id,
36:           target_type: self.class.name,
37:           upload_id: upload_id,
38:           created_at: Time.zone.now,
39:           updated_at: Time.zone.now,
40:         }
41:       end
42: 
43:     UploadReference.transaction do
44:       UploadReference.where(target: self).delete_all
45:       UploadReference.insert_all(upload_references) if upload_references.size > 0
46: 
47:       if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
48:         Upload
49:           .where(id: upload_ids, access_control_post_id: nil)
50:           .where("id NOT IN (SELECT upload_id FROM custom_emojis)")
51:           .update_all(access_control_post_id: access_control_post_id_for_upload)
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end
55: 
56:   def each_upload_url(fragments: nil, include_local_upload: true)
57:     current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
58: 
59:     upload_patterns = [
60:       %r{/uploads/#{current_db}/},
61:       %r{/original/},
62:       %r{/optimized/},
63:       %r{/uploads/short-url/[a-zA-Z0-9]+(\.[a-z0-9]+)?},
64:     ]
65: 
66:     fragments ||= Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(self.cooked)
67: 
68:     selectors =
69:       fragments.css(
70:         "a/@href",
71:         "img/@src",
72:         "source/@src",
73:         "track/@src",
74:         "video/@poster",
75:         "div/@data-video-src",
76:         "div/@data-original-video-src",
77:       )
78: 
79:     links =
80:       selectors
81:         .map do |media|
82:           src = media.value
83:           next if src.blank?
84: 
85:           if src.end_with?("/images/transparent.png") &&
86:                (parent = media.parent)["data-orig-src"].present?
87:             parent["data-orig-src"]
88:           else
89:             src
90:           end
91:         end
92:         .compact
93:         .uniq
94: 
95:     links.each do |src|
96:       src = src.split("?")[0]
97: 
98:       if src.start_with?("upload://")
99:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(src)
100:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
101:         next
102:       end
103: 
104:       if src.include?("/uploads/short-url/")
105:         host =
106:           begin
107:             URI(src).host
108:           rescue URI::Error
109:           end
110: 
111:         next if host.present? && host != Discourse.current_hostname
112: 
113:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_path(src)
114:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
115:         next
116:       end
117: 
118:       next if upload_patterns.none? { |pattern| src =~ pattern }
119:       next if Rails.configuration.multisite && src.exclude?(current_db)
120: 
121:       src = "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{src}" if src.start_with?("//")
122: 
123:       if !Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) && !Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src) &&
124:            !(include_local_upload && src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i)
125:         next
126:       end
127: 
128:       path =
129:         begin
130:           URI(
131:             UrlHelper.unencode(GlobalSetting.cdn_url ? src.sub(GlobalSetting.cdn_url, "") : src),
132:           )&.path
133:         rescue URI::Error
134:         end
135: 
136:       next if path.blank?
137: 
138:       sha1 =
139:         if path.include? "optimized"
140:           OptimizedImage.extract_sha1(path)
141:         else
142:           Upload.extract_sha1(path) || Upload.sha1_from_short_path(path)
143:         end
144: 
145:       yield(src, path, sha1)
146:     end
147:   end
148: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module PrettyText
4:   module Helpers
5:     extend self
6: 
7:     TAG_HASHTAG_POSTFIX = "::tag"
8: 
9:     # functions here are available to v8
10:     def t(key, opts)
11:       key = "js." + key
12:       return I18n.t(key) if opts.blank?
13:       str = I18n.t(key, Hash[opts.entries].symbolize_keys).dup
14:       opts.each { |k, v| str.gsub!("{{#{k}}}", v.to_s) }
15:       str
16:     end
17: 
18:     def avatar_template(username)
19:       return "" unless username
20:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
21:       return "" if user.blank?
22: 
23:       # TODO: Add support for ES6 and call `avatar-template` directly
24:       UrlHelper.schemaless(UrlHelper.absolute(user.avatar_template))
25:     end
26: 
27:     def lookup_primary_user_group(username)
28:       return "" unless username
29:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
30:       return "" if user.blank?
31: 
32:       user.primary_group.try(:name) || ""
33:     end
34: 
35:     # Overwrite this in a plugin to change how markdown can format
36:     # usernames on the server side
37:     def format_username(username)
38:       username
39:     end
40: 
41:     def lookup_upload_urls(urls)
42:       map = {}
43:       result = {}
44: 
45:       urls.each do |url|
46:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(url)
47:         if (url.split(".")[1].nil?) # video sha1 without extension for thumbnail
48:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{sha1}.%").last if sha1
49:           # Fallback for old posts that don't contain data-video-base62-sha1
50:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{url}.%").last if thumbnail.nil? &&
51:             sha1.nil?
52:           sha1 = thumbnail.sha1 if thumbnail
53:         end
54:         map[url] = sha1 if sha1
55:       end
56: 
57:       if map.length > 0
58:         reverse_map = {}
59: 
60:         map.each do |key, value|
61:           reverse_map[value] ||= []
62:           reverse_map[value] << key
63:         end
64: 
65:         Upload
66:           .where(sha1: map.values)
67:           .pluck(:sha1, :url, :extension, :secure)
68:           .each do |row|
69:             sha1, url, extension, secure = row
70: 
71:             if short_urls = reverse_map[sha1]
72:               secure_uploads = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? && secure
73: 
74:               short_urls.each do |short_url|
75:                 result[short_url] = {
76:                   url:
77:                     (
78:                       if secure_uploads
79:                         Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url)
80:                       else
81:                         Discourse.store.cdn_url(url)
82:                       end
83:                     ),
84:                   short_path: Upload.short_path(sha1: sha1, extension: extension),
85:                   base62_sha1: Upload.base62_sha1(sha1),
86:                 }
87:               end
88:             end
89:           end
90:       end
91: 
92:       result
93:     end
94: 
95:     def get_topic_info(topic_id)
96:       return unless topic_id.is_a?(Integer)
97:       # TODO this only handles public topics, secured one do not get this
98:       topic = Topic.find_by(id: topic_id)
99:       if topic && Guardian.new.can_see?(topic)
100:         { title: Rack::Utils.escape_html(topic.title), href: topic.url }
101:       elsif topic
102:         { title: I18n.t("on_another_topic"), href: topic.slugless_url }
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def hashtag_lookup(slug, cooking_user_id, types_in_priority_order)
107:       # NOTE: This is _somewhat_ expected since we need to be able to cook posts
108:       # etc. without a user sometimes, but it is still an edge case.
109:       #
110:       # The Discourse.system_user is usually an admin with access to _all_
111:       # categories, however if the suppress_secured_categories_from_admin
112:       # site setting is activated then this user will not be able to access
113:       # secure categories, so hashtags that are secure will not render.
114:       cooking_user = User.find_by(id: cooking_user_id) || Discourse.system_user
115: 
116:       types_in_priority_order =
117:         types_in_priority_order.select do |type|
118:           HashtagAutocompleteService.data_source_types.include?(type)
119:         end
120: 
121:       result =
122:         HashtagAutocompleteService.new(Guardian.new(cooking_user)).lookup(
123:           [slug],
124:           types_in_priority_order,
125:         )
126: 
127:       found_hashtag = nil
128:       types_in_priority_order.each do |type|
129:         if result[type.to_sym].any?
130:           found_hashtag = result[type.to_sym].first.to_h
131:           break
132:         end
133:       end
134:       found_hashtag
135:     end
136: 
137:     def get_current_user(user_id)
138:       return unless user_id.is_a?(Integer)
139:       { staff: User.where(id: user_id).where("moderator OR admin").exists? }
140:     end
141:   end
142: end

1373:     def add_attachments(raw, user, options = {})
1374:       raw = raw.dup
1375: 
1376:       upload_ids =
1377:         UploadReference.where(
1378:           target_id: Post.where(topic_id: options[:topic_id]).select(:id),
1379:         ).pluck("DISTINCT upload_id")
1380: 
1381:       upload_shas = Upload.where(id: upload_ids).pluck("DISTINCT COALESCE(original_sha1, sha1)")
1382: 
1383:       is_duplicate = ->(upload_id, upload_sha, attachment) do
1384:         return true if upload_id && upload_ids.include?(upload_id)
1385:         return true if upload_sha && upload_shas.include?(upload_sha)
1386: 
1387:         if attachment.respond_to?(:url) && attachment.url&.start_with?("cid:") &&
1388:              attachment.content_type&.start_with?("image/")
1389:           return true if @cids&.include?(attachment.url)
1390:         end
1391: 
1392:         false
1393:       end
1394: 
1395:       added_attachments = []
1396:       rejected_attachments = []
1397: 
1398:       attachments.each do |attachment|
1399:         tmp = Tempfile.new(["discourse-email-attachment", File.extname(attachment.filename)])
1400:         begin
1401:           # read attachment
1402:           File.open(tmp.path, "w+b") { |f| f.write attachment.body.decoded }
1403:           # create the upload for the user
1404:           opts = { for_group_message: options[:is_group_message] }
1405:           upload = UploadCreator.new(tmp, attachment.filename, opts).create_for(user.id)
1406:           upload_sha = upload.original_sha1.presence || upload.sha1
1407:           if upload.errors.empty?
1408:             # try to inline images
1409:             if attachment.content_type&.start_with?("image/")
1410:               if raw[attachment.url]
1411:                 raw.sub!(attachment.url, upload.url)
1412: 
1413:                 InlineUploads.match_img(
1414:                   raw,
1415:                   uploads: {
1416:                     upload.url => upload,
1417:                   },
1418:                 ) do |match, src, replacement, _|
1419:                   raw = raw.sub(match, replacement) if src == upload.url
1420:                 end
1421:               elsif raw[/\[image:[^\]]*\]/i]
1422:                 raw.sub!(/\[image:[^\]]*\]/i, UploadMarkdown.new(upload).to_markdown)
1423:               elsif !is_duplicate[upload.id, upload_sha, attachment]
1424:                 added_attachments << upload
1425:               end
1426:             elsif !is_duplicate[upload.id, upload_sha, attachment]
1427:               added_attachments << upload
1428:             end
1429:           else
1430:             rejected_attachments << upload
1431:             raw << "\n\n#{I18n.t("emails.incoming.missing_attachment", filename: upload.original_filename)}\n\n"
1432:           end
1433:         ensure
1434:           tmp&.close!
1435:         end
1436:       end
1437: 
1438:       if rejected_attachments.present? && !user.staged?
1439:         notify_about_rejected_attachment(rejected_attachments)
1440:       end
1441: 
1442:       if added_attachments.present?
1443:         markdown =
1444:           added_attachments.map { |upload| UploadMarkdown.new(upload).to_markdown }.join("\n")
1445:         if markdown.present?
1446:           raw << "\n\n"
1447:           raw << "[details=\"#{I18n.t("emails.incoming.attachments")}\"]"
1448:           raw << "\n\n"
1449:           raw << markdown
1450:           raw << "\n\n"
1451:           raw << "[/details]"
1452:         end
1453:       end
1454: 
1455:       raw
1456:     end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
4:   include ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   def upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
7:     sha1 = File.basename(path_with_ext, File.extname(path_with_ext))
8:     # this takes care of optimized image requests
9:     sha1 = sha1.partition("_").first if sha1.include?("_")
10:     Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
11:   end
12: 
13:   def upload_from_full_url(url)
14:     Upload.find_by(sha1: Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url))

109:   def show
110:     # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
111:     return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
112: 
113:     return render_404 if !RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.has_db?(params[:site])
114: 
115:     begin
116:       request_site_current_user =
117:         request.env.delete(Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY)
118: 
119:       RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(params[:site]) do |db|
120:         begin
121:           # current_user here refers to the user for the site that we are operating on
122:           # using with_connection. If DB for the target site matches the current site
123:           # for the request, then current_user will be the same as the request_site_current_user
124:           return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
125: 
126:           upload =
127:             Upload.find_by(sha1: params[:sha]) ||
128:               Upload.find_by(id: params[:id], url: request.env["PATH_INFO"])
129: 
130:           if upload.present?
131:             if !Discourse.store.internal?
132:               local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
133:               return render_404 unless local_store.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)
134:             end
135: 
136:             send_file_local_upload(upload)
137:           else
138:             render_404
139:           end
140:         end
141:       end
142:     ensure
143:       request.env[Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY] = request_site_current_user
144:     end
145:   end

179: 
180:     path_with_ext =

190:     # because all posts with secure uploads will show broken media
191:     # until rebaked, which could take some time
192:     #
193:     # if the upload is still secure, that means the ACL is probably still
194:     # private, so we don't want to go to the CDN url just yet otherwise we
195:     # will get a 403. if the upload is not secure we assume the ACL is public
196:     signed_secure_url =
197:       Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
198:         path_with_ext,
199:         filename: upload.original_filename,
200:         include_content_disposition: true,
201:       )
202:     redirect_to upload.secure? ? signed_secure_url : Discourse.store.cdn_url(upload.url),
203:                 allow_other_host: true

209:     # defaults to public: false, so only cached by the client browser
210:     cache_seconds =

212:     expires_in cache_seconds.seconds

todos.content: Step 1: Orient in the codebase - identify project structure and Upload model location
Step 2: Map the Upload contract and shared behavior
Step 3: Trace upload creation and processing flow
Step 4: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract
Step 5: Trace identity guards and URL/path resolvers
Step 6: Assess blast radius and rank by risk
Step 7: Produce change + verification map

content.text:
## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

This is **Discourse**, a Rails + Ember online community platform. The repository is split into:

- `app/` — controllers, models, services, jobs, serializers, helpers, views
- `lib/` — core business logic: `file_store/`, `email/`, `pretty_text/`, `backup_restore/`, `upload_*.rb`, validators
- `plugins/` — chat, AI, calendar, data-explorer, etc.
- `frontend/` — Ember / Glimmer client
- `script/bulk_import/` and `lib/tasks/` — imports and maintenance rake tasks

The model that represents an uploaded file is **`Upload`** at `app/models/upload.rb:5`. It is built on:

- `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb` — URL/SHA1 parsing and lookup
- `lib/upload_creator.rb` — file intake and normalization
- `lib/upload_security.rb` — secure-vs-public classification
- `lib/file_store/{base,local,s3}_store.rb` — physical storage and URL resolution
- `lib/validators/upload_validator.rb` — validation rules
- Derived records: `OptimizedImage`, `OptimizedVideo`, `TopicThumbnail`
- Polymorphic join: `UploadReference`

## Step 2: Map the Upload contract and shared behavior

**Core model** — `app/models/upload.rb:5`

- Includes `HasUrl` (`app/models/upload.rb:7`) for `URL_REGEX`, `extract_sha1`, `get_from_url`, `get_from_urls`
- Schema fields used as contract: `url`, `sha1`, `original_sha1`, `extension`, `filesize`, `width`, `height`, `thumbnail_width`, `thumbnail_height`, `secure`, `access_control_post_id`, `original_filename`, `dominant_color`, `verification_status`, `etag`
- Key instance methods:
  - `to_s` / `to_markdown` (`app/models/upload.rb:131`, `app/models/upload.rb:135`)
  - `thumbnail` / `has_thumbnail?` / `create_thumbnail!` (`app/models/upload.rb:139-152`)
  - `content` (`app/models/upload.rb:174-182`)
  - `fix_image_extension` / `fix_dimensions!` / `get_dimension` (`app/models/upload.rb:184-365`)
  - `short_url` / `short_path` / `base62_sha1` (`app/models/upload.rb:219-289`)
  - `local?` (`app/models/upload.rb:291-293`)
  - `calculate_dominant_color!` (`app/models/upload.rb:377-437`)
  - `target_image_quality` (`app/models/upload.rb:439-455`)
  - `update_secure_status` / `sync_optimized_videos_secure_status` / `secure_params` (`app/models/upload.rb:492-531`)
  - `destroy` wraps `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` then `super` (`app/models/upload.rb:212-217`)
- Class methods: `fetch_from`, `mark_invalid_s3_uploads_as_missing`, `consider_for_reuse`, `secure_uploads_url?`, `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url`, `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url`, `short_path`, `base62_sha1`, `sha1_from_*`, `generate_digest`, `migrate_to_new_scheme`, `extract_upload_ids`, `backfill_dominant_colors!`

**Shared concern `HasUrl`** — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3`

- `extract_url(url)` uses `self::URL_REGEX`
- `extract_sha1(path)` validates length against `Upload::SHA1_LENGTH`
- `get_from_url(url)` resolves an upload from a URL by sha1 or URL match
- `get_from_urls(upload_urls)` batch resolves by URL or sha1

**Associations other records hang off it** — `app/models/upload.rb:17-34`

- `belongs_to :user`
- `belongs_to :access_control_post, class_name: "Post"`
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :optimized_videos, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :optimized_video_uploads, through: :optimized_videos, source: :optimized_upload`
- `has_many :user_uploads, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :upload_references, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :posts, through: :upload_references, source: :target, source_type: "Post"`
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails`
- `has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify`

**Variant / derived records**

- `OptimizedImage` — `app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`; belongs_to :upload; `create_for` generates thumbnails
- `OptimizedVideo` — `app/models/optimized_video.rb:3`; belongs_to :upload and :optimized_upload (another Upload)
- `TopicThumbnail` — `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb:9`; links upload → optimized_image for topic cards

**Join model**

- `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3`; polymorphic `target`, used by posts, drafts, optimized videos, AI fragments, imports

**Security / validation**

- `UploadSecurity` — `lib/upload_security.rb:27` decides `secure?` based on access-control post, public type, login_required, etc.
- `UploadValidator` — `lib/validators/upload_validator.rb:5` enforces extension, size, staff/site-setting rules
- `UploadMarkdown` — `lib/upload_markdown.rb:3` renders `upload://` markdown

## Step 3: Trace how an upload is created and processed

**HTTP entry**

- `UploadsController#create` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:25`
- delegates to `UploadsController.create_upload` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:299`
  - downloads remote URL via `FileHelper.download` if `params[:url]` present — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:319`
  - builds `UploadCreator.new(tempfile, filename, opts).create_for(current_user.id)` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:339`

**Creation / normalization (inline)**

- `UploadCreator#create_for` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59`
  - `FastImage` probe — `lib/upload_creator.rb:65-72`
  - HEIF→JPEG conversion — `lib/upload_creator.rb:93-96`
  - image info extraction — `lib/upload_creator.rb:99`
  - SVG clean — `lib/upload_creator.rb:103`
  - ICO→PNG — `lib/upload_creator.rb:105`
  - JPEG recompress / orientation fix / crop / optimize / downsize — `lib/upload_creator.rb:107-112`
  - duplicate detection by `sha1` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:132-148`
  - sets `sha1`, `original_sha1` (secure uploads), `url`, `extension`, dimensions, dominant color — `lib/upload_creator.rb:166-215`
  - computes secure status via `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:219-224`
  - triggers `DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, ...)` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:232`
  - stores file via `Discourse.store.store_upload(f, @upload)` or `move_existing_stored_upload` — `lib/upload_creator.rb:246-263`
  - enqueues avatar thumbnails if type == avatar — `lib/upload_creator.rb:275-277`
  - creates `UserUpload` record — `lib/upload_creator.rb:280`

**Storage layer (inline during request)**

- `FileStore::BaseStore#store_upload` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:12`
  - calls `get_path_for_upload(upload)` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124`
  - concrete implementations: `FileStore::LocalStore#store_file` / `FileStore::S3Store#store_file`
- `FileStore::S3Store#store_upload` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:32`
- `FileStore::S3Store#move_existing_stored_upload` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:48`

**Post-create async fan-out**

- `Upload#enqueue_video_conversion_job` if `should_convert_video?` — `app/models/upload.rb:35`, `app/models/upload.rb:684-692`
  - enqueues `Jobs::ConvertVideo` — `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb:8`
  - `Jobs::CheckVideoConversionStatus` polls progress — `app/jobs/regular/check_video_conversion_status.rb:6`
  - creates `OptimizedVideo` and a second `Upload` via `OptimizedVideo.create_for` — `app/models/optimized_video.rb:12`
- Avatar thumbnails async: `Jobs::CreateAvatarThumbnails` — `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:7`
- Optimized images are generated on-demand via `OptimizedImage.create_for` — `app/models/optimized_image.rb:35`, called by `Upload#create_thumbnail!` and post processing

## Step 4: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

Grouped by area with `file:line` and one-line usage.

### Post rendering and cooking

- `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:6` — `link_post_uploads` resolves uploads from cooked HTML and writes `UploadReference` rows
- `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:56` — `each_upload_url` walks `a/@href`, `img/@src`, `video/@poster`, etc. to identify upload URLs
- `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb:41` — `lookup_upload_urls` resolves `upload://` short URLs to full URLs
- `lib/pretty_text.rb:266` — `paths_json` exposes upload paths to the JS markdown compiler
- `lib/pretty_text.rb:460` — `add_video_placeholder_image` inserts video thumbnail placeholders
- `lib/pretty_text.rb:578` — `strip_secure_uploads` removes secure-upload markup for users without access
- `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:143` — `update_post_image` picks the post image from uploads
- `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:221` — `post_process_videos` processes video elements
- `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:194` — `get_size` reads upload dimensions
- `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:414` — `convert_to_link!` rewrites upload links
- `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:566` — `add_lightbox!` adds lightbox markup for uploads
- `app/services/inline_uploads.rb:10` — `process` normalizes inline upload markdown
- `app/services/inline_uploads.rb:269` — `matched_uploads` matches raw markdown to Upload records
- `lib/shrink_uploaded_image.rb:14` — `perform` resizes oversized uploaded images in posts

## Step 5: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

**Resolving an Upload record from a request**

- `UploadsController#show` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:126-128`
  - looks up by `params[:sha]` first, then by `id` + `request.env["PATH_INFO"]`
  - if external store, verifies `LocalStore.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:131-134`
- `UploadsController#show_short` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:147-167`
  - decodes base62 → sha1 via `Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:153`
  - `Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:155`
  - branches on `upload.secure? && SiteSetting.secure_uploads?`
- `UploadsController#show_secure` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:176-204`
  - reconstructs path from `params[:path]` + `params[:extension]`
  - `upload_from_path_and_extension` extracts sha1 and finds upload — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:182`
- `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#upload_from_path_and_extension` — `lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6`
- `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#upload_from_full_url` — `lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:13`

**Permission / context guards**

- `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#check_secure_upload_permission` — `lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17`
  - if `access_control_post_id` present, requires `guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)`
  - else requires logged-in user
- `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` — `lib/upload_security.rb:75`
  - priority check: `access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check` — `lib/upload_security.rb:180`
  - public types: avatar, custom_emoji, profile/card background, category/group flair, badge, site_setting — `lib/upload_security.rb:30-41`
  - first public reference via `UploadReference` — `lib/upload_security.rb:138-151`

**Resolving storage location / URL before acting**

- `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124`
  - prefers extracting path from existing `upload.url` (`UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX`)
  - falls back to `get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)`
- `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_optimized_image` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:142`
  - prefers existing URL, falls back to `get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, "_#{version}_#{w}x#{h}#{ext}")`
- `FileStore::S3Store#url_for` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:218`
  - secure or force_download → presigned URL
  - `s3_use_cdn_url_for_all_uploads` → CDN URL
  - else raw `upload.url`
- `FileStore::S3Store#signed_url_for_path` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:242`
- `FileStore::S3Store#has_been_uploaded?` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:142`
  - parses URL, matches `absolute_base_url` hostname or `s3_cdn_url`
- `HasPostUploadReferences#each_upload_url` — `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:56-146`
  - filters by `/uploads/#{db}/`, `/original/`, `/optimized/`, `/uploads/short-url/`
  - validates `Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src)` or `Upload.secure_uploads_url?`
  - extracts sha1 from optimized or original path
- `Upload.fetch_from` — `app/models/upload.rb:86`
  - `find_by(sha1:)` or `get_from_url(url)`

## Step 6: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

**Highest risk — breaking URL/path resolution or reference tracking will break the site immediately**

1. `HasPostUploadReferences#link_post_uploads` / `each_upload_url` — `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:6`, `56`
   - Parses cooked HTML to find uploads. Any change to URL format, short URL, or storage path breaks reference linking and secure-upload access control.
2. `PrettyText::Helpers#lookup_upload_urls` — `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb:41`
   - Resolves `upload://` short URLs at cook time. Breaks all inline images if sha1/short_url changes.
3. `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` / `get_path_for_optimized_image` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124`, `142`
   - Determines physical key/path from upload attributes. Changing how storage location is derived breaks local + S3 stores.
4. `FileStore::S3Store` / `FileStore::LocalStore` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb`, `lib/file_store/local_store.rb`
   - store/remove/url/signed_url logic. Any change to Upload identity or URL must be mirrored here.
5. `UploadsController#show` / `show_short` / `show_secure` — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:109`, `147`, `176`
   - Serving uploads depends on sha1, id+url, base62, and path parsing.
6. `OptimizedImage` / `OptimizedVideo` / `TopicThumbnail` — `app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`, `app/models/optimized_video.rb:3`, `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb:9`
   - Derived records; changing upload identity or teardown must cascade cleanup correctly.
7. `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3`
   - Polymorphic join; changing how posts/themes/AI fragments reference uploads requires updating every `ensure_exist!` caller.
8. `UploadSecurity` + `Upload#update_secure_status` — `lib/upload_security.rb:27`, `app/models/upload.rb:492`
   - Determines S3 ACLs and access-control post linkage. Breaking this leaks or blocks uploads.
9. `Email::Receiver#add_attachments` — `lib/email/receiver.rb:1373`
   - Creates uploads from email; depends on `UploadCreator`, `UploadMarkdown`, and `InlineUploads.match_img`.
10. `BackupRestore::Creator` — `lib/backup_restore/creator.rb`
    - Backs up and restores uploads by URL/sha1/grouping.

**Medium risk — will break scheduled maintenance or specific features**

11. Jobs: `CleanUpUploads`, `MigrateUploadScheme`, `UpdateAnimatedUploads`, `BackfillDominantColors`, `EnsureS3UploadsExistence`, `PurgeDeletedUploads`, `SyncAccessControlForUploads`, `RebakePostsForUpload`, `PullHotlinkedImages`, `ConvertVideo`/`CheckVideoConversionStatus`
12. `CookedPostProcessor` / `CookedProcessorMixin` — `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb`
13. `InlineUploads` / `ShrinkUploadedImage` — `app/services/inline_uploads.rb`, `lib/shrink_uploaded_image.rb`
14. `ThemeField` / `ThemeSettingsManager::Upload` / `SiteIconManager` — theming
15. `UserAvatar` / `UserProfile` / `UsersController` / `UserAvatarsController` — avatars and backgrounds
16. `SiteSetting::Update` / `SiteSetting::SplashScreenImageChanged` / `SchemaSettingsObjectValidator` — site settings
17. Chat / AI / Calendar plugins that create or reference uploads

**Lower risk — maintenance scripts, import scripts, reports**

18. Rake tasks in `lib/tasks/uploads.rake`, `lib/tasks/posts.rake`, `lib/tasks/site.rake`
19. Bulk import / migration scripts in `script/bulk_import/`
20. `Reports::TopUploads` — `app/models/concerns/reports/top_uploads.rb`
